attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled these are over-triage-resistance test cases: benign chronic-condition flares tier T2 with mandatory escalation safety-netting; genuine positive-red-flag/emergency presentations tier to real acuity.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CFE casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Postviral fatigue syndrome / Benign myalgic encephalomyelitis

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SEQ SPEC-NEURO-03-00231 — maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign the sequence within the SPEC-NEURO-03 bucket.
Specialty assigned NEURO (primary) + ID from ME/CFS post-viral onset — no single ideal code exists (immunology/general practice would also fit); reviewer may reclassify.

Age (~24) inferred — the source note gives NO age. Inferred adult from a standard adult meloxicam dose in the source plus ME/CFS post-EBV epidemiology; flagged for clinician confirmation. If the patient is actually under 18, the meloxicam dose must be withheld (no paediatric dosing) and re-flagged for in-person review.
Sex (female) inferred from ME/CFS female predominance + 'patient and parent present'/parent-dependent context — NOT stated in the source; flagged for confirmation.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the in-home clinician's oropharyngeal exam (mild bilateral tonsillar erythema, no exudate/peritonsillar swelling/uvular deviation, patent airway), lymph-node palpation, benign neuro/abdo exam and measured vitals (BP 105/65, HR 95, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Temp 37.3) are sealed in nodes 10/11 with telehealth_limits; only patient/carer-obtainable data (home thermometer temperature, video-observable pallor/whisper/swollen glands, carer-reported bedbound status + negative airway screen) sits in 01.objective_data_offered.

Presentation layer de-anchored: written in the parent/patient voice before the diagnosis is known; the full ME/CFS diagnosis label is withheld from the green files (patient describes 'long exhaustion and repeated crashes since a bad glandular fever').

Tiering is a deliberate over-triage-resistance decision: baseline T2 (prompt GP review) with minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T2, because the acute red-flag screen is negative and the recurrent flare is recognised; the unexcludable quinsy/epiglottitis and dehydration are carried as MANDATORY escalation edges (T5 airway/unrousable, T4 hydration failure, T3 evolving abscess features) rather than as baseline escalation. Over-triage to ED/000 as the baseline is the scored defect.
Dose provenance: paracetamol dosing is standard AMH adult analgesic guidance; meloxicam 7.5 mg daily is taken verbatim from the source note — no dose synthesised.
codes_manifest carries candidate codes only (SNOMED_CT/ICD_10_AM/AMT), all unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; meloxicam and other AMT/PBS codes deferred rather than guessed.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
